headline: The educators converge on agent orchestration: meta-harnesses that route work across multiple coding models, paired with hardening for the security gaps those agents open.
body: The recommended channels lead with orchestration layers that treat coding models as interchangeable workers: Cole Medin walks through Omnigent, a meta-harness routing tasks across Claude Code, Codex, and Pi with human-in-the-loop governance and cross-device session persistence [4], while Cyril Imhof covers Claude Fable 5 alongside harness engineering and AI loops for regulated industries [2]. IBM Technology pairs this with the security and productivity angles: AI as a pair programmer for debugging and code review [5], Kagenti's identity-based, delegation-chain approach to securing multi-agent systems against invisible breaches [3], and a debate over trusting AI agents with password management amid a 200-plus CVE Patch Tuesday [1]. Supporting demos extend the agent thread, with Bright Data's Rafael Levi showing agents that silently fail web searches by hitting CAPTCHAs and falling back to stale data [11], Jack Roberts mapping a seven-level Hermes agent progression and Microsoft Scout's proactive Teams and calendar automation [6][8], and MiniMax M3 arriving as an open-weights coding model with 1-million-token context and Anthropic and OpenAI compatible APIs [13]. Better Stack adds a local, privacy-focused angle with Voicebox, an open-source voice studio offering cloning, text-to-speech, and dictation as an ElevenLabs alternative [0].
